vision: A single Claude Code marketplace (zenhive) distributing a set of independent, per-repo-toggleable plugins — orchestration (harness, workflow, tasks, review, delegation, portfolio, git-commit), development discipline (code-quality, dev-discipline, marketplace-hygiene), and per-language dev tooling (elixir, elixir-volt, phoenix, elixir-workflows). A marketplace is the distribution unit; a plugin is the isolation unit. Most skill bodies are auto-synced from the canonical ~/.claude/includes/*.md source via scripts/skill-include-map.sh, so the includes stay the single source of truth and the plugins are their distribution channel. Supersedes the older deltahedge marketplace (ZenHive/claude-marketplace-elixir), rebranded to the org name and restructured so orchestration and per-language concerns are distinct plugins.

skill-ref-hygiene.description: Sweep stale plugin/skill references left by the deltahedge→zenhive consolidation and the staged-review→review plugin rename.

stack-hygiene.description: Portfolio-wide developer-hygiene plugins shipped from the marketplace and enabled across the onchain-stack repos (dependency advisories, etc.).

v0_1.name: zenhive cutover
v0_1.description: Proves the deltahedge→zenhive rename + concern-split lands cleanly: all plugins resolve, includes→skills sync works from the new repo, and the old marketplace is fully unregistered.

task.title: Sweep bare `staged-review` plugin-name and path references after staged-review→review rename
acceptance_criteria: grep -rn 'staged-review' across the marketplace returns only intentional archaeology (CHANGELOG + 'deleted v1.5+' notes)
No broken `plugins/staged-review/...` paths remain in any SKILL.md, README, command, or template
Canonical includes fixed at source and SKILL.md regenerated via sync-skills-from-includes.sh (not hand-edited)
out_of_scope: Colon-qualified plugin:skill rename (already done in commit 569b463)
The harness plugin's two self-synced skills
task.body:
The `staged-review` plugin was renamed to `review` (directory `plugins/staged-review/` no longer exists). A prior sweep fixed all colon-qualified `staged-review:<skill>` invocation tokens, but ~30 BARE references remain stale across ~15 files: plugin-name prose (e.g. "the `staged-review` SessionStart hook", "the `staged-review` plugin") and broken path references (e.g. `plugins/staged-review/templates/auto-merge.md`, `plugins/staged-review/scripts/check-unaudited-commits.sh`).

Many live in the canonical `~/.claude/includes/*.md` sources (so fix the include, then run `scripts/sync-skills-from-includes.sh`), not the generated SKILL.md bodies. The two unmapped hand-authored review SKILL.md files (`plugins/review/skills/audit-review`, `.../code-review`) need direct edits.

Rename bare `staged-review` -> `review` and `plugins/staged-review/...` -> `plugins/review/...`. EXCLUDE genuine archaeology: CHANGELOG.md historical entries and "Replaces ... (deleted v1.5+)" notes that intentionally reference the old name as a historical record — judge each occurrence, this is not a blind global replace.

task.title: Add ecosystem-detecting dependency-advisory SessionStart hook (stack-wide, one shared plugin)
acceptance_criteria: A single shared script (one source of truth) ships in a marketplace plugin and is invoked via SessionStart hooks.json using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, mirroring plugins/review's hook wiring
Script detects ecosystem per repo: mix.lock -> mix_audit + hex.audit; native/**/Cargo.toml -> cargo audit; package.json -> npm audit
Hook is non-blocking and silent when no advisories, when tooling is missing, and when the watermark is fresh — never errors or stalls a session
Watermark-gated so the nag is periodic, not every-session, following mpp's .sdk-watch.json checked_at pattern
On findings, output suggests a triage flow that files sanitized rmap tasks per the repo's disclosure policy (no exploit detail in committed files)
Install path documented: mix_audit added where expected; cargo-audit prerequisite noted; hook no-ops cleanly pre-install
out_of_scope: Copying mpp's refs-pull SessionStart hook or its parity-diff (sdk-delta-watch) machinery — the stack wraps deps, it does not reimplement upstreams
Per-repo duplicated copies of the script (must be one shared plugin-shipped source)
Actually triaging/fixing any specific advisory — this task ships the detector + nag, not the remediation
Zig-dependency auditing for onchain_js beyond npm (note as a follow-up if no clean Zig advisory tool exists)
task.body:
Ship ONE shared SessionStart hook that nags (never blocks) when a repo's dependencies carry known security advisories, installed across the seven onchain-stack repos via the marketplace plugin mechanism — not copied per-repo.

Motivation: mpp's `.claude/hooks/sdk-watch-reminder.sh` SessionStart hook proves the "watermark-gated staleness nag -> suggest a triage skill, quiet when fresh" pattern works well. But mpp's hook is repo-local and parity-diff-driven because mpp REIMPLEMENTS a protocol (it vendors upstream reference SDKs under refs/ and git-diffs them). The onchain stack does NOT reimplement — it depends on pinned packages (Hex deps everywhere; revm/alloy/solang Rust crates in onchain_evm; npm/Zig in onchain_js). So the transferable slice is the nag MECHANISM repointed at dependency ADVISORIES, not ref-diffing. Do not copy mpp's refs-pull hook or parity-watch machinery.

Build it as a single ecosystem-detecting script shipped by an existing portfolio/dev-discipline-style plugin (model the wiring on `plugins/review/hooks/hooks.json` + `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/check-unaudited-commits.sh`). One script, auto-adapting per repo by detecting which manifests are present:
  - `mix.lock` present  -> mix_audit (mix.lock CVEs) + `mix hex.audit` (retired packages) — the uniform layer across ALL seven repos
  - `native/**/Cargo.toml` present -> `cargo audit` (RustSec) — onchain_evm only
  - `package.json` / `npm.lock` present -> `npm audit` — onchain_js only

Quiet when clean or when tooling is absent (degrade gracefully, never error a session). Watermark-gate the nag the way mpp does (a small state file with a checked_at threshold) so it surfaces periodically, not every session. When advisories are found, the hook output should suggest running a triage skill/flow that files findings as rmap tasks in the affected repo (under that repo's security-disclosure policy — see critical-rules section "NEVER BROADCAST AN UNPATCHED VULNERABILITY": sanitized rmap body, detail in a private advisory).

Tooling is NOT yet present: `mix_audit` is not a dep in any repo, and `cargo-audit` is not installed. Part of this task is documenting/wiring the install path (add `:mix_audit` as a dev/test dep where the hook expects it; note the `cargo install cargo-audit` prerequisite for onchain_evm) — the hook must no-op cleanly until the tooling exists rather than spewing errors.

The seven repos and their enablement live in onchain-workspace.md; enabling the owning plugin in each repo's `.claude/settings.json` (same `enabledPlugins` shape mpp uses for `elixir@zenhive`) is how the one hook reaches all seven.
